keyPoints: 1. Problem Statement: Find the first occurrence of a pattern in a text.
2. Best Case: O(n/m) where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern.
3. Worst Case: O(n*m) when all characters in the pattern are different from the characters in the text.
4. Horspool's Algorithm: Shift the pattern to the right until a match is found or the end of the text is reached.
5. Key Insight: Use the last character of the pattern to shift the pattern left.
6. How it Works: Preprocess the pattern to create a shift table, then iterate through the text, shifting the pattern as needed.
